title: /tfo
text: /tfo Record keystrokes in a Uniface application running in character mode to an ASCII file. /tfo { = FileName } Arguments FileName —optional file name; maximum of 12 characters. Letters, digits, and underscores (_) are allowed; the first character must be a letter. If no name is given, the Shell .tfo is used. If no extension is specified, .tfo (Test File Output) is assumed. Description Record all the keystrokes used in a Uniface application in an ASCII file. Only keystrokes are recorded in the TFO file. Mouse movement and clicks are not recorded. When you use the /tfo switch, a copy of all output which normally occurs on the message line also occurs in the message frame. The recorded file can be played back with the /tfi switch to reproduce the session exactly as it was recorded, with the keyboard input coming from a file instead of the keyboard. Note: The application must be running in character mode (that is, $GUI=$CHR ). Related Links /tfi Play back the recorded keystrokes stored in a test file output file. Was this topic helpful?
